description: T169 ReviewSubmissionGateway MUST call eligibilityService.check() at executionOrder=1 â€” before any write, before SETNX, before audit. INVERTS FLOW-08 T79 audit-first pattern. SILENT_FAILURE: audit record created for an ineligible submission. Compliance tools treat audit records as evidence of valid events. An ineligible attempt with an audit record is indistinguishable from a rejected valid attempt â€” corrupts compliance reporting. Passes all unit tests with mocked eligibility response.
resolution: eligibilityService.check() call appears BEFORE auditService.storeDocument() in T169. Line index eligibility < line index audit. DPO conflict: conflictsWith FLOW-08-T79-audit-first-pattern. Absent eligibility gate = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: auditService.storeDocument() called before eligibilityService.check() in T169
T169 creates audit record for an ineligible submission
eligibility check is not the first operation in T169
passConditions: eligibilityService.check() call appears before auditService.storeDocument() in T169
T169 returns DataProcessResult.failure('not_eligible') before reaching audit call

description: T169 MUST compute reviewId server-side as SHA-256 hash(tenantId+':'+reviewerId+':'+targetEntityId+':'+targetEntityType). MUST NOT read event.reviewId. SILENT_FAILURE: accepting reviewId from payload allows callers to (1) supply a known reviewId to overwrite an existing review record, (2) supply a fabricated ID to bypass idempotency check, (3) target specific SETNX slots. The SETNX key and document ID are the same server-derived hash â€” no separate lookup needed. Passes all tests when test fixtures provide valid hashes.
resolution: T169 calls createHash('sha256').update(tenantId+':'+reviewerId+':'+targetEntityId+':'+targetEntityType).digest('hex'). event.reviewId NEVER read. Reading event.reviewId = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T169 reads event.reviewId or event.id for SETNX key
T169 uses event.reviewId as the storeDocument _id
reviewId derived from caller-supplied data instead of server-computed hash
passConditions: T169 computes reviewId = SHA-256(tenantId+':'+reviewerId+':'+targetEntityId+':'+targetEntityType)
SETNX key and storeDocument _id both use server-computed derivedReviewId
event.reviewId never referenced in T169

description: T169 MUST validate rating [1,5] at executionOrder=2 â€” BEFORE SETNX at executionOrder=3. Idempotency keys are a finite resource. SILENT_FAILURE: SETNX called before rating validation consumes the idempotency slot for an invalid rating=6 submission. The user cannot submit a valid review for the same entity because the slot is permanently consumed. No error returned to user â€” SETNX silently 'succeeds'. Only visible in production when users complain they cannot resubmit.
resolution: Rating validation check (rating >= 1 && rating <= 5) appears BEFORE SETNX call in T169. Line index validation < line index SETNX. SETNX before validation = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: SETNX called before rating validation in T169
T169 acquires idempotency slot before verifying rating is in [1,5]
invalid rating (e.g. rating=6) submission consumes SETNX slot before rejection
passConditions: rating validation (rating >= 1 && rating <= 5) appears before SETNX call in T169
invalid rating returns DataProcessResult.failure before idempotency slot is consumed

description: T169 ineligible submissions MUST return early without creating any audit record. Audit trail must record only valid events. SILENT_FAILURE: early return after eligibility check still reaches auditService.storeDocument() due to missing return statement. All unit tests pass because mocked audit service records the call silently. Compliance audit shows review attempts from ineligible users as if they were valid submissions.
resolution: T169: if (!eligibility.eligible) return DataProcessResult.failure('not_eligible', ...) â€” hard return, never reaches audit. Audit call reachable on ineligible path = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T169 ineligible path reaches auditService.storeDocument() due to missing return
audit record created for ineligible review attempt
T169 does not hard-return immediately on eligibility check failure
passConditions: T169 returns DataProcessResult.failure('not_eligible') immediately after eligibility check fails
no audit record created on ineligible path
hard return statement prevents audit call from being reached

description: T169 MUST emit ReviewSubmissionCompleted alongside ReviewAccepted (executionOrder=5) â€” NOT after T170 moderation. The submitter's synchronous wait ends when eligibility and validity are confirmed. SILENT_FAILURE: ReviewSubmissionCompleted emitted after ReviewPublished causes the submitter to wait for async moderation (potentially minutes) before receiving acknowledgment. Moderation is time-decoupled. ReviewSubmissionCompleted is the response contract â€” ReviewPublished is a separate async signal.
resolution: T169 emits both ReviewAccepted AND ReviewSubmissionCompleted at executionOrder=5. ReviewSubmissionCompleted emitted from T170 or later = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: ReviewSubmissionCompleted emitted from T170 or after moderation completes
T169 does not emit ReviewSubmissionCompleted at executionOrder=5
submitter waits for async moderation before receiving acknowledgment
passConditions: T169 emits both ReviewAccepted AND ReviewSubmissionCompleted at executionOrder=5
ReviewSubmissionCompleted emitted from T169 only — never from T170 or later

description: T169 MUST call storeDocument(review record to xiigen-reviews) BEFORE enqueue(ReviewAccepted). DNA-8 outbox pattern. SILENT_FAILURE: enqueue first means downstream consumers may process the event before the review record exists. Lookup-by-reviewId in T170 returns null. Moderation engine processes a review that doesn't exist yet â€” null dereference in T170 production but not in unit tests where T170 is mocked.
resolution: T169 calls storeDocument() then enqueue(). Line index storeDocument < line index enqueue. enqueue before storeDocument = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T169 calls enqueue(ReviewAccepted) before storeDocument(review record)
review record does not exist in xiigen-reviews when T170 processes ReviewAccepted
DNA-8 outbox order violated in T169
passConditions: T169 calls storeDocument() then enqueue(ReviewAccepted)
line index storeDocument < line index enqueue in T169

description: T169 MUST NOT pass event.reviewId or event.id to the SETNX key or document ID. The idempotency slot and document ID MUST both derive from the server-computed hash. SILENT_FAILURE: A caller who knows the hash of an existing review can supply it as reviewId to bypass the SETNX check (SETNX returns false â†’ duplicate â€” but if the ID is accepted as a write target, the record is overwritten). Two code paths accept event.reviewId: (1) direct hash usage, (2) passed to storeDocument as the _id field.
resolution: Both SETNX key AND storeDocument _id must use the server-computed derivedReviewId. event.reviewId must never appear in SETNX or storeDocument calls. Any usage = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: event.reviewId used as SETNX key in T169
event.reviewId passed as storeDocument _id field in T169
reviewId from event payload appears in SETNX or storeDocument arguments
passConditions: SETNX key uses server-computed derivedReviewId only
storeDocument _id uses server-computed derivedReviewId only
event.reviewId never appears in SETNX or storeDocument arguments

description: T170 ReviewModerationEngine MUST implement exactly 3 moderation paths. INVERTS FLOW-08 binary moderation. SILENT_FAILURE: binary moderation (confidence < threshold â†’ REJECT) silently discards borderline but valid content. A review with confidence=0.72 between thresholds is auto-rejected instead of queued for human review. No error â€” the review is simply never published. The submitter sees 'ReviewRejected' for valid content. DPO conflict annotation required: conflictsWith FLOW-08-binary-moderation.
resolution: T170 implements: if (confidence >= passThreshold) â†’ ReviewPublished; else if (confidence < rejectThreshold) â†’ ReviewRejected; else â†’ ReviewFlaggedForHuman(PENDING). Binary if/else = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T170 implements binary APPROVE/REJECT without UNCERTAIN path
confidence between passThreshold and rejectThreshold routes to ReviewRejected
ReviewFlaggedForHuman event not emitted by T170
dpoConflict annotation conflictsWith FLOW-08-binary-moderation absent
passConditions: T170 implements: confidence >= passThreshold → ReviewPublished
T170 implements: confidence < rejectThreshold → ReviewRejected
T170 implements: else → ReviewFlaggedForHuman with status=PENDING
dpoConflict annotation conflictsWith FLOW-08-binary-moderation present

description: T170 UNCERTAIN path MUST emit ReviewFlaggedForHuman with status=PENDING â€” NEVER status=REJECTED. SILENT_FAILURE: UNCERTAIN mapped to REJECTED means the review receives a permanent terminal state. The reviewer is told their review was rejected â€” but the actual reason is only that the AI was uncertain, not that the content violated policy. A moderator never sees it. The review is permanently lost. Only distinguishable from a legitimate rejection by inspecting the moderation record â€” no surface-level error.
resolution: T170 UNCERTAIN path: emit('ReviewFlaggedForHuman', { reviewId, status: 'PENDING' }). storeDocument with status=PENDING. status=REJECTED on UNCERTAIN path = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T170 UNCERTAIN path emits ReviewFlaggedForHuman with status=REJECTED
UNCERTAIN moderation result produces terminal REJECTED state
borderline content permanently rejected without human review opportunity
passConditions: T170 UNCERTAIN path emits ReviewFlaggedForHuman with {status: 'PENDING'}
storeDocument called with status=PENDING on UNCERTAIN path
PENDING status routes record to human moderator queue

description: T171 ReputationScoreAggregator MUST subscribe to BOTH ReviewPublished AND ReviewRetracted. Additive-only aggregation permanently inflates scores. SILENT_FAILURE: a reviewer submits a 5-star review (score updates to 4.8), then retracts it. Without ReviewRetracted handler, the score remains 4.8 â€” the retracted review permanently inflates reputation. No error. Only detectable by comparing stored reviews (RETRACTED count) against reputation score history. First additive-subtractive pattern in XIIGen (FLOW-05/06 are additive-only).
resolution: T171 declares @EventPattern('ReviewPublished') onPublished() AND @EventPattern('ReviewRetracted') onRetracted(). Both handlers call recalculate(). Missing ReviewRetracted handler = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T171 only subscribes to ReviewPublished without ReviewRetracted handler
retracted reviews remain in reputation aggregate calculation
T171 does not call recalculate() on ReviewRetracted event
passConditions: T171 declares @EventPattern('ReviewPublished') onPublished() calling recalculate()
T171 declares @EventPattern('ReviewRetracted') onRetracted() calling recalculate()
both handlers present and both call recalculate()

description: T171 recalculate() MUST fetch reviews with status=PUBLISHED filter â€” NEVER include RETRACTED, PENDING, or REJECTED reviews. SILENT_FAILURE: fetching all reviews without status filter includes retracted reviews in the aggregate. Even if a running counter is updated on retraction, the full-recalculate path (which runs on both add and remove) re-includes retracted reviews. Score inflation survives retraction. Flagged and pending reviews also included â€” content not yet approved affects reputation.
resolution: T171 recalculate() queries xiigen-reviews with filter { status: 'PUBLISHED', targetEntityId }. No status filter in query = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T171 recalculate() queries xiigen-reviews without status=PUBLISHED filter
RETRACTED, PENDING, or REJECTED reviews included in reputation aggregate
full-recalculate path re-includes non-PUBLISHED reviews
passConditions: T171 recalculate() queries with filter {status: 'PUBLISHED', targetEntityId}
only PUBLISHED reviews contribute to reputation score calculation

description: T171 MUST apply Math.max(1.0, Math.min(5.0, rawScore)) before storeDocument. Star-rating scale [1.0, 5.0] â€” NOT quality score [0,1] (do NOT confuse with FLOW-06). SILENT_FAILURE: heavy recency weighting with sparse data (1 recent 1-star review, 10 old 5-star reviews) produces weighted average below 1.0. Raw score 0.8 written to reputation record. Downstream consumers render '0.8 stars' â€” impossible on a 1-5 scale. Score out of range only visible after specific data patterns emerge in production.
resolution: T171 applies clamp: const score = Math.max(1.0, Math.min(5.0, rawScore)) before storeDocument. Named check: reputation_score_clamped_1_to_5. No clamp before storeDocument = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T171 stores rawScore without Math.max(1.0, Math.min(5.0, rawScore)) clamp
reputation score outside [1.0, 5.0] range stored to xiigen-reputation-scores
clamp not applied before storeDocument in T171
passConditions: T171 applies score = Math.max(1.0, Math.min(5.0, rawScore)) before storeDocument
stored reputation score is always in range [1.0, 5.0]
named check: reputation_score_clamped_1_to_5

description: T172 MUST set revision_allowed:true ONLY for content_policy rejection reason. not_owner and already_responded MUST carry revision_allowed:false. SILENT_FAILURE: always setting revision_allowed:true tells a non-owner they may revise their response. The non-owner submits a revision which is rejected again for not_owner â€” consuming another idempotency slot. Multiple iterations: each revision attempt by a non-owner burns a revision slot (revision-1, revision-2...) until slots exhausted. The non-owner is trapped in a revision loop with no real path to success.
resolution: T172 emitRejection(): reason === 'content_policy' â†’ revision_allowed:true; reason === 'not_owner' OR 'already_responded' â†’ revision_allowed:false. Always-true revision_allowed = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T172 sets revision_allowed:true for not_owner rejection reason
T172 sets revision_allowed:true for already_responded rejection reason
T172 always sets revision_allowed:true regardless of rejection reason
passConditions: T172 emitRejection(): reason === 'content_policy' → revision_allowed:true
T172 emitRejection(): reason === 'not_owner' → revision_allowed:false
T172 emitRejection(): reason === 'already_responded' → revision_allowed:false

description: T172 ReviewResponseOrchestrator MUST check ownership (reviewerId === review.ownerId) at executionOrder=1 â€” before SETNX at executionOrder=2. SILENT_FAILURE: SETNX before ownership check consumes the response slot for a non-owner. A legitimate owner attempting to respond later finds the slot consumed â€” they cannot respond to their own review because a non-owner burned the idempotency slot. SETNX key = hash(tenantId+reviewId+'response') is global per review â€” only one response permitted.
resolution: T172 checks review.ownerId === responderId BEFORE SETNX call. Line index ownership check < line index SETNX. SETNX before ownership check = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T172 calls SETNX before ownership check
idempotency slot consumed for non-owner response attempt before rejection
ownership check (review.ownerId === responderId) not at executionOrder=1
passConditions: T172 checks review.ownerId === responderId before SETNX call

description: T172 ReviewResponseOrchestrator MUST NOT emit reputation.updated. Reputation aggregation is exclusively owned by T171 (triggered by ReviewPublished/ReviewRetracted events). SILENT_FAILURE: T172 emitting reputation.updated causes double-counting â€” T171 recalculates on ReviewPublished, T172 recalculates again on response submission. A response to a review does not change the review's rating â€” reputation score should be unchanged. Double update creates phantom score changes with no new review data.
resolution: T172 emits ReviewResponsePublished OR ReviewResponseRejected only. No reputation.updated emission. Any reputation.updated emission from T172 = BUILD_FAILURE.

T172 triggers reputation recalculation on response submission

description: T172 MUST call storeDocument(response record) BEFORE enqueue(ReviewResponsePublished or ReviewResponseRejected). DNA-8 outbox pattern. SILENT_FAILURE: enqueue first creates a race where notification consumers fire before the response record exists. A notification service delivering 'your response was published' fires before the record is queryable. The owner's dashboard shows no response when they click through from the notification.
resolution: T172 calls storeDocument() then enqueue(). Line index storeDocument < line index enqueue on all paths. enqueue before storeDocument = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T172 revision submissions MUST use a NEW_KEY_VARIANT SETNX key: hash(tenantId+':'+reviewId+':response-revision-1'). Not the same key as the original response. SILENT_FAILURE: using the same SETNX key as the original response means a revision attempt after content_policy rejection returns 'already_responded' (SETNX key already consumed). The user was told revision_allowed:true but the system prevents them from revising. Contradiction: UI shows revision form, system rejects with duplicate. No error code distinguishes original duplicate from revision duplicate.
resolution: T172 revision path uses deriveRevisionKey() â†’ hash(tenantId+':'+reviewId+':response-revision-1'). Original key = hash(tenantId+':'+reviewId+':response'). Same key for both = BUILD_FAILURE.

deriveRevisionKey() not implemented — revision uses hash(tenantId+reviewId+'response')

T172 original response uses hash(tenantId+':'+reviewId+':response') as SETNX key

description: Review records, reputation scores, and response records are PRIVATE (per-reviewer per-entity). T171 xiigen-reputation-scores is PRIVATE â€” per-tenant aggregate, not public GLOBAL. FREEDOM config keys are GLOBAL (platform-level config). Audit records are PRIVATE (per-submission). PRIVATE records must never be stored as GLOBAL. tenantId must come from AsyncLocalStorage, never from request parameters.
